title: viola turkestanica subsp. rupestris
intro: Viola turkestanica subsp. rupestris is a plant species within the violet family, known for its small, delicate flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Viola turkestanica subsp. rupestris, including teas and infusions, can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and skin irritations.
